visual: Plus-size woman in kitchen with a mug, looking out window
hook: When did you stop choosing yourself?
caption:
I see this every day in my work. Women who give until there's nothing left for themselves. and they don't even notice when it happened.

Can you relate?
question: Can you relate?

visual: Couple sitting at opposite ends of a couch, evening light
hook: You're not fighting. But something is missing.
caption:
Many couples come into my office and say "but we don't fight, why am I so lonely?" I tell them: the absence of conflict isn't presence.

Reach out if you feel lonely in your relationship. We can shift it.
question: Reach out if you feel lonely in your relationship.

visual: Brunette short-hair woman on a couch, hand on chin
hook: You can love him and still feel lonely.
caption:
I hear this every week: "I love him, so why do I feel so alone?" We need connection and intimacy. We need to learn how to ask for what we need and inspire our partners to respond.

Do you want to learn how?
question: Do you want to learn how?

visual: Woman dancing alone in a sunlit living room
hook: This is how she came back to herself.
caption:
Every woman I've worked with who came back to herself did it through the body. Not through fixing or trying to think her way through it. But through moving, breathing, choosing herself again and again.

Do you live mostly in your body or in your head?
question: Do you live mostly in your body or in your head?

visual: Older blonde woman in bedroom, arms crossed, lamp lit
hook: It's not that you don't care.
caption:
Women tell me "something must be wrong with me, I don't want him anymore." I say: it's not that you don't care. You've been giving without receiving for years. And maybe you haven't found your erotic voice. Maybe you never had a chance to explore your sexuality and just counted on hormones for desire.

Things shifted. You need to learn how to turn yourself on now.

Want to know more?
question: Want to know more?

visual: Woman outdoors at sunset, cardigan, calm
hook: You're allowed to want more.
caption:
I had a client say "I think I want more" like a confession. There is nothing wrong with wanting more. Pleasure. Closeness. Yourself.

But as women we feel so guilty about it!

I say, keep on asking!

Is it hard for you to ask?
question: Is it hard for you to ask?

visual: Older woman seated cross-legged in meditation, candle glow
hook: Your body is still speaking.
caption:
Your body has been talking to you the whole time. The tension. The fatigue. The shutdown. She's not betraying you, she's trying to protect you. But how often do you override her?

Say, go to bed too late? Push through when you're tired? Eat crappy food?

What is she trying to tell you?
question: What is she trying to tell you?

visual: Plus-size woman in kitchen, darker variant
hook: When did you stop choosing yourself?
caption:
I ask my clients this and they almost always cry. Because the answer is "years ago" and no one noticed, including them.

Do you relate?

Say with me "I choose me"
question: Do you relate?

visual: Brunette woman in kitchen, laughing, holding a mug
hook: There is nothing wrong with wanting more.
caption:
So many women in my work feel guilty for wanting more. More closeness. More pleasure. More aliveness. Wanting more is a sign you want to be fully engaged with life. You want to be fully alive.

What is the more you want?
question: What is the more you want?

visual: Couple eating dinner in low warm light, quiet
hook: Everything looks fine. That's the part that hurts.
caption:
The hardest relationship cases I see aren't the ones in crisis. They're the ones where everything looks fine. kids, house, marriage, but something is dying inside.

Can you relate?
question: Can you relate?

visual: Woman standing in front of an open wardrobe
hook: You don't hate your body. You've stopped listening to her.
caption:
I see this in every client who calls herself broken. You don't hate your body. You've stopped listening to her. She's been talking and talking and now maybe she's screaming.

What is she trying to tell you?
question: What is she trying to tell you?

visual: Woman lying awake in bed, partner turned away
hook: The loneliest place can be next to someone you love.
caption:
Women come to me saying "I feel more alone next to him than I do alone." That's the part no one warns you about. We think if we're in love, everything will be great. No, we need to learn how to create deep connection and reach out to each other.

Want to learn how? Comment 'connection'
question: Want to learn how? Comment 'connection'

visual: Brunette woman looking in a bathroom mirror
hook: no one told you desire can disappear
caption:
No one warned us this could happen. Desire doesn't leave in a day. It leaves in a thousand small ones. And then one morning you can't find it.

It hasn't disappeared for good! We can bring it back. Comment 'desire' to find out how
question: Comment 'desire' to find out how

visual: Woman walking outdoors at sunset, cardigan and jeans
hook: You're not done.
caption:
I want every woman over 40 to hear this: you're not done. There is so much more to life and love in store for you!

Are you ready?

Are you willing to take the first step?
question: Are you ready?

visual: Brunette woman in kitchen, square variant
hook: When did you stop choosing yourself?
caption:
When did you stop choosing yourself? Not in one big moment. In a thousand small ones, the times you said yes when you wanted to say no.

What would it feel like to choose YOU?
question: What would it feel like to choose YOU?

visual: Brunette woman on couch, alternate angle
hook: You can love him and still feel lonely.
caption:
I want to say this clearly: you can love him and still feel lonely. The two are not opposites. Connection requires intention and communication.

When was the last time you spend at least a minute looking into each others eyes?
question: When was the last time you spend at least a minute looking into each others eyes?

visual: Brunette woman in kitchen with a mug, looking out the window
hook: you miss being wanted
caption:
Women tell me "I don't even know what I miss anymore." I tell them: you miss being wanted. Not the touching. The feeling of being chosen.

Can you relate?
question: Can you relate?

visual: Woman alone at a kitchen table with a coffee cup
hook: This is what disconnection looks like.
caption:
This is what disconnection actually looks like. Not fights. Not drama. Just two people sharing logistics and a bed. The quiet kind of distance is the hardest to name.

Painful, right?

Comment 'enough' if you want to know how to change this.
question: Comment 'enough' if you want to know how to change this.

visual: Older blonde woman in bedroom, longer hook overlay
hook: It's not that you don't care. but you don't want sex.
caption:
I hear this so often: "I love him. I just don't want sex." It's not that you don't care. You've been performing for years and your body finally said no. Your body is smart.

And maybe it's not that you don't want sex. Maybe it's that you don't want the kind of sex that's on offer?

Yes or no?
question: Yes or no?

visual: Woman on a couch in golden light, smiling softly
hook: This version of you? She's just beginning.
caption:
I want every woman to know this: this version of you isn't the end. She's just beginning. You're not at the close of anything. You're at the start.

Who are you becoming?
question: Who are you becoming?

visual: Brunette woman in kitchen with mug, vertical variant
hook: When did you stop choosing yourself?
caption:
Many women tell me they "lost themselves somewhere along the way." They didn't lose her. They stopped choosing her. The choice is still yours.

Are you choosing yourself now?
question: Are you choosing yourself now?

visual: Woman in front of wardrobe, square format variant
hook: You don't hate your body. You've stopped listening to her.
caption:
Your body has been calling you home the whole time. The tightness. The numbness. The exhaustion. She's not betraying you, she's begging you to come back.

Can you stop for a moment and listen to her? Ask your body 'how are you doing, darling? what do you need from me?'
question: Can you stop for a moment and listen to her?

visual: Brunette woman in kitchen laughing, square variant
hook: There is nothing wrong with wanting more.
caption:
I had a client say "wanting more feels selfish." I told her: wanting more is the body remembering she was alive. There is nothing selfish about it.

What do you want more of?
question: What do you want more of?

visual: Woman in bathrobe by a rainy window, holding a mug
hook: Maybe you're not tired of sex. Maybe you're tired of performing.
caption:
Most women I work with don't have low desire. They have low capacity to perform. And they've been performing for years. There is a difference.

Can you relate?
question: Can you relate?

visual: Brunette woman at a bathroom mirror, longer hook overlay
hook: no one told you one day you'll stop wanting sex with your partner
caption:
No one prepared us for this. The day you realise you don't want sex with your partner anymore. It feels like betrayal... until you understand it's a signal, not a verdict.

Are you ready to find your desire again?

I bet it's still there. Waiting to be awakened.
question: Are you ready to find your desire again?

visual: Older woman seated meditating, simpler background
hook: Your body is still speaking.
caption:
Your body hasn't gone silent. She's been speaking the whole time. In tightness. In fatigue. In the longing you can't name. Are you listening yet?

Are you? Ask her 'how are you doing my darling? how can I take care of you today?'
question: Are you listening yet?

visual: Brunette woman in kitchen with a mug, wistful
hook: I miss feeling wanted.
caption:
I had a woman in my office say "I miss feeling wanted more than I miss sex." That sentence stayed with me for weeks. The ache underneath the ache.

When did you last feel wanted?
question: When did you last feel wanted?

visual: Brunette woman on couch, larger format variant
hook: You can love him and still feel lonely.
caption:
I see it every week. Women who love their partner and still feel painfully alone. Loving someone is NOT the same as being met by them.

Can you relate?

Comment 'me' to learn how to shift this.
question: Comment 'me' to learn how to shift this.
